_meta.description: Hard memory stores exact facts that must never be altered during generation. These are domain-specific constants, confirmed term mappings, and verified values.
_meta.usage: This file is loaded in Phase A (GATHER) as a runtime asset. Every entry here is treated as ground truth. If user notes conflict with hard_memory, flag the conflict but present the hard_memory value as the established standard.
_meta.maintenance: Entries are added when a fact is confirmed. Entries are never modified without explicit user approval.

critical_terms._note: CN-EN term rules confirmed from own papers. For context-dependent terms, keep approved variants and avoid mistranslations rather than forcing a single fixed rendering.

基坑.note: Context-dependent. 'deep excavation' and 'excavation' are preferred in Papers 02-04; 'foundation pit' appears in Papers 01, 05. See term_glossary.yml.

围护结构.note: Context-dependent. 'retaining wall' is preferred in Papers 02-03 when referring to diaphragm walls specifically.

物理约束.note: Context-dependent. Prefer 'physics-informed constraint' when used with loss terms or regularization.

standard_units._note: Standard units used in geotechnical AI papers. Always use these unless the user specifies otherwise.

notation_conventions._note: Symbol conventions the user consistently uses across papers.

evaluation_metrics._note: Standard metrics and their abbreviations as used in the user's papers.

model_names._note: User's own model names. Always capitalize and format exactly as listed.

journal_formatting._note: Journal-specific formatting rules the user has encountered. Add entries as new journals are targeted.

Computers_and_Geotechnics.figure_citation: Fig. X or Figure X (both accepted)
